events.title: Paint a collaborative wall mural at Lucchesi Vineyards
events.description: A hands-on wall mural workshop will be offered at Lucchesi Vineyards in Grass Valley California. Gary Graham, a well-known muralist, will instruct the various procedures required to create a unique work of art. This workshop will allow you to participate in all stages of creating a wall mural, from design and drawing to the painting itself., Each day of this six-day workshop will focus on a different element of the wall mural creation. You can attend one day, a weekend, or all of the sessions. Each day is a separate ticketed event, so you can pick and choose for your schedule and interests., The workshop dates and topics are as follows:July 11 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 12 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 18 - Paint (underpainting), July 19 - Paint (underpainting), July 25 - Paint (overpainting), July 26 - Paint (overpainting + glazing)

The show, which features original paintings and sculptures, will be on display at the downtown Nevada City art supply store through August 16th., Wonder Docent will host an opening reception for In the Night Dimension and Those You Meet at Twilight on Saturday, June 20th from 6 to 8 pm.

events.title: The Creative Labyrinth 101: Nature and Creativity Journal Making
events.description: The Creative Labyrinth: Nature & Creativity Journal Making, Create a beautiful hard-cover journal or sketchbook from start to finish in this hands-on book arts workshop., Using the versatile Australian Hinge Binding, you’ll learn how to construct a professional-quality journal with a hard cover and multiple removable signatures. This unique binding style allows pages to be added, rearranged, or replaced over time, making it ideal for journaling, sketching, note-taking, nature observations, or creative projects., No previous bookbinding experience is needed. All materials and supplies are provided, and you’ll leave with a finished handcrafted book ready to use., What You’ll LearnThe anatomy of a hard-cover book, How to create and assemble book covers, Folding and preparing signatures, Australian Hinge Binding techniques, Methods for adding, removing, and rearranging pages, What You’ll CreateOne hard-cover journal, Ten removable signatures (approximately 40 pages), A versatile book that can be used as a journal, sketchbook, creative notebook, or nature journal, Optional Follow-Up Workshop, Participants may continue their journey in a second workshop, where the finished books become vessels for creative exploration through prompts, imagery, storytelling, and reflective practices inspired by nature and creativity., Class Details, Date: July 12, Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75, Class Size: Maximum 8 participants, Optional Follow-Up Session: July 19, 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m. ($75)

Featuring the voice talents of James Caan, John Cho, Darren Criss, Lucy Liu, James Marsden, and Chloë Grace Moretz, director Isao Takahata’s final film was nominated for an Academy Award® and hailed as a masterpiece by critics.

events.title: Build Your Own Skateboard – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
events.description: Build your dream skateboard at our summer camp! Learn woodworking skills and make a custom board that stands out with your unique laser cut designs., Using a vacuum press and multiple layers of veneer, create a custom board with a kick . You will learn wood equipment safety and use of band saws, panel saw, router, sanders as well as learn and use laser cutters. Finish up by laser etching your design onto your deck then stain, seal and assemble trucks, wheels and bearings. Your end product will be a high-end custom skateboard!, Our camps are designed to be both fun and educational, with experienced instructors who are passionate about sharing their skills and knowledge with young people. We take great care to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where every child can feel valued and supported., Advanced Wood Fabrication processes:Vacuum Forming, Hardwood Veneers, Router, Bandsaw, Drilling, CNC Laser Cutting, Ages 12+, 4-Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM each day, $295, Class 4 minimum/6 maximum participants. (please note: if attendance does not meet minimum, class will be rescheduled)

events.description: Join us for an adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! events.title: Intro to Screen Printing
events.description: Intro to Screen Printing, Step into the studio for a beginner-friendly introduction to the bold, hands-on world of screen printing. In this guided workshop, you’ll discover how ink, a screen, and a squeegee can come together to create clean, professional prints — and you’ll walk away with a custom Curious Forge t-shirt that’s entirely your own., Through demonstration and hands-on practice, you’ll learn the fundamentals of the screen printing process from start to finish. We’ll cover fabric and ink types, proper printing form, squeegee technique, equipment maintenance, and ink curing — everything you need to understand the craft and feel confident doing it again on your own., No experience necessary. This class is designed for curious beginners, creative minds ready to try something new, and anyone who’s ever wanted to know how their favorite printed tee was made. It’s also family friendly — teens and adults are welcome., Details:Total Class Duration: 2 Hours, Who: Beginners, families, and the print-curious, Price: $40 class fee + $20 materials = $60 total, Ages: Adults and Teens 12+, Class Size: Minimum 4 / Maximum 8 participants, What to Wear: Please wear clothing you don’t mind getting ink on — screen printing is a hands-on process and stray ink happens to the best of us., Please note: All classes require a minimum number of participants and may be rescheduled if that minimum is not met. If this happens, we’ll let you know in advance and work with you to get you into the next available session.

events.description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Buffalo Gals are a beloved Americana and old-time string band based in Nevada City, California. Known for their traditional bluegrass twang, country rags, and vibrant harmonies, the group frequently performs at local venues, farmers' markets, and community fundraisers. Margo Meredith: fiddle & vocals; Jonathan Meredith: guitar; Ann Meigs: mandolin; Michael "Banjolio" Bremer: banjo & resonator guitar; Philip Wright: Bass

events.title: Playwriting Group
events.description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

events.title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
events.description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

events.description: Let's have a party and raise some money to support dance in Nevada City! Expect nothing, receive all your heart desires. Good times!, Join us for an evening of cabaret style entertainment - song, dance, poetry, and surprise! Then push the chairs aside and vogue with us and dance the night away!, Dance Because artists mix and mingle with you as we prepare to get to work on our second annual Nevada Theatre showcase coming in January 2027. Our goal is to have a party and raise $5000 to support production expenses for this upcoming show - costumes, rehearsal space, promotion. Our January, 2026 Nevada Theatre show featured 18 choreographers and 45 dancers and sold out three nights! We're building a movement here, folks, and it's just getting better. Help us fulfill our vision to celebrate our human-ness and our vital connections through the dance experience., Featured artists in the cabaret include: Shari Brookler, Kirstin Brown, Elizabeth Castañeda, Isadora Goldschneider, Azriel LaMarca, Leo Lauchere, Juliet Lin, Mary McDonnell, Delana Motter, Cassie Powell, Erin Reynolds, Sister Magic, and Trina Spiller, Tickets $35 for the whole evening.

events.title: The Creative Labyrinth 102: Filling the Pages
events.description: The Creative Labyrinth: Filling the Pages, Session Two: prerequisite Creative Labyrinth 101, Bring your handmade journal to life in this playful and inspiring follow-up workshop., Using a combination of collage, simple book arts techniques, drawing, painting, writing, and collected imagery, you’ll begin transforming the blank pages of your Australian Hinge journal into a personal creative companion., Participants will explore themes and ideas through intuitive prompts, curated materials, and creative exercises designed to spark curiosity and imagination. Whether you’re creating a nature journal, visual diary, inspiration book, sketchbook, collection of favorite quotes, or something entirely your own, you’ll leave with new pages completed and plenty of ideas for continuing your book at home., No previous art experience is necessary. This workshop emphasizes exploration, experimentation, and creative play rather than artistic perfection., What You’ll LearnSimple methods for developing themes and creative direction, Collage techniques using paper, images, text, and found materials, Basic pocket-making techniques for storing notes, sketches, and treasures, Ways to combine writing, drawing, painting, and collected imagery on the page, Ideas for building an ongoing creative journaling practice, What You’ll CreateSeveral completed journal pages, Handmade pockets and interactive elements, A personalized framework for continuing your journal beyond the workshop, Class Details, Date: July 19, Time: 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., Ages: Adults & teens 13+, Skill Level: Beginner-friendly, Fee: $75

events.venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
events.description: 7/19/2026- Sophie, a quiet girl working in a hat shop, finds her life thrown into turmoil when she is literally swept off her feet by a handsome but mysterious wizard named Howl. The vain and vengeful Witch of the Waste, jealous of their friendship, puts a curse on Sophie and turns her into a 90-year-old woman. On a quest to break the spell, Sophie climbs aboard Howl’s magnificent moving castle and into a new life of wonder and adventure. But as the true power of Howl’s wizardry is revealed, Sophie finds herself fighting to protect them both from a dangerous war of sorcery that threatens their world. Featuring the voice talents of Lauren Bacall, Christian Bale, Billy Crystal, Blythe Danner, Emily Mortimer, and Jean Simmons.

events.title: Tom Rush
events.description: The Center for the Arts presents Tom Rush live on July 22., A cornerstone of the American folk movement, Tom Rush has spent over 50 years captivating audiences with his expressive voice, masterful guitar work, and deeply personal songwriting. From shaping the folk revival of the 1960s to influencing generations of singer-songwriters, his music continues to connect with listeners old and new., Known for his storytelling and engaging stage presence, Rush creates a live experience that feels both intimate and timeless. His performances blend humor, heart, and musicianship, offering audiences an evening that is as moving as it is memorable., Special guest Brendan Cleary opens the evening.

events.description: Rediscover the hits that defined a generation., Alternative rock pioneers The Smithereens formed in New Jersey in 1980. The band continues to tour worldwide and captivate audiences with their signature guitar-driven sound., The Smithereens perform live in Grass Valley on Friday, July 24, 2026. Doors open at 7:00 PM and the show begins at 8:00 PM. Seats are limited, so secure your spot for a night of classic alternative rock.

events.title: STEAM Exploration Saturdays
Paint a collaborative wall mural at Lucchesi Vineyards
events.description: A hands-on wall mural workshop will be offered at Lucchesi Vineyards in Grass Valley California. Gary Graham, a well-known muralist, will instruct the various procedures required to create a unique work of art. This workshop will allow you to participate in all stages of creating a wall mural, from design and drawing to the painting itself., Each day of this six-day workshop will focus on a different element of the wall mural creation. You can attend one day, a weekend, or all of the sessions. Each day is a separate ticketed event, so you can pick and choose for your schedule and interests., The workshop dates and topics are as follows:July 11 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 12 - Design process + drawing (including color, perspective, scale), July 18 - Paint (underpainting), July 19 - Paint (underpainting), July 25 - Paint (overpainting), July 26 - Paint (overpainting + glazing)

events.title: Make your own Hand-Carved Wooden Spatula
events.description: Hand-Carved Wooden – Spoons and Spatulas (see tickets for project), 3 hours class – , There’s something deeply satisfying about shaping wood with your own two hands., In this beginner-friendly workshop, you’ll start with a simple block of wood and transform it into a beautiful, functional wooden utensil using traditional hand tools. Under expert guidance, you’ll learn how to safely and confidently use an axe, carving knife, and scoop knife to shape, hollow, and refine your piece., This class is perfect for anyone curious about traditional woodworking or wanting to slow down and experience the rhythm of working with hand tools. No prior experience is necessary — just bring your curiosity and a willingness to learn., By the end of class, you’ll leave with a hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula and the foundational skills to continue carving on your own., What You’ll Create , One hand-carved wooden spoon or spatula (depending on the class ticket you choose), What You’ll Learn, • Safe and proper use of an axe, • Knife carving techniques, • How to use a scoop knife for hollowing, • Foundational greenwood carving skills, Experience Level, Beginners— No experience needed, Ages, Adults, and Teens 13+ may participate when accompanied by an adult., (The accompanying adult may choose to observe or purchase a ticket to participate.), All materials and tools are provided.

Make that special pot that shows the sudden uptick in your skills. Enjoy playing with clay and making ceramic pottery with your own unique flair. All clay and materials included.

events.title: Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert
events.description: Under the direction of Cheryl Woldseth, the 60-member Nevada County Concert Band invites you to join us for the July Pioneer Park Picnic Pops Concert as part of our summer series of outdoor performances. Make it a family tradition to enjoy a picnic concert with the Nevada County Concert Band! Expect a lively program, including toe-tapping marches, iconic Broadway hits, contemporary tunes, and much more. Bring your friends, family, neighbors, and clubs to the park for a fun-filled evening of music with the official Nevada County band! The concert is free, open to the public, and accessible to all, making it a perfect outing for families. Leashed dogs are welcome. Pack a picnic dinner or treat yourself to hot dogs and ice cream from our vendors. Don't forget your lawn chair or blanket, and settle in for a delightful evening of music at the park.

events.title: 10,000 Maniacs
events.description: Celebrating 45 years as one of America’s most beloved alternative rock bands, 10,000 Maniacs bring to the stage a career-spanning show that blends classics and new music. From unforgettable hits like “These Are Days,” “Like the Weather,” “More Than This”, and “Because the Night, the band continues to inspire with poetic lyrics, lush melodies, and dynamic performances. , Fronted by Mary Ramsey and joined by founding members Jerome Augustyniak, John Lombardo, Dennis Drew, and Steven Gustafson, along with guitarist Ben Medina, 10,000 Maniacs invite you to experience a night of timeless music that has captivated audiences for generations.

events.title: The Wind Rises
events.venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
events.description: 7/26 - Jiro dreams of flying and designing beautiful airplanes, inspired by the famous Italian aeronautical designer Caproni. Nearsighted and unable to be a pilot, he becomes one of the world’s most accomplished airplane designers, experiencing key historical events in an epic tale of love, perseverance and the challenges of living and making choices in a turbulent world. A late career masterpiece from Director Hayao Miyazaki, THE WIND RISES is a spellbinding film beyond compare.

events.title: Stuffy Animal Adventures – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
events.description: Bring your favorite Stuffy!, Step into the world of pretend play as we turn our classroom into a veterinarian’s office! Kids will give their stuffed animals check-ups, create their own doctor kits to take home, and learn how to care for their plush patients. Along the way, we’ll explore basic biology and anatomy through fun crafts, creative play, and hands-on activities.

events.title: Wonder Circle Mythic Art & Nature Journey Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
events.description: The Wonder Circle – Youth Maker XD Summer Camp, Some children see the world a little differently — they notice the light through the leaves, the shape of a shadow, the story hiding inside a stone. The Wonder Circle was made for them., Ages 9 to 12, 4 Day Camp | 3 hours a day, Monday – Thursday, 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM each day, $225, This small-group creative experience is designed for children aged 8–12 who are naturally curious, imaginative, and sensitive to the world around them. Unlike traditional art classes focused on technique or “how to draw,” The Wonder Circle invites children into a calm, story-rich environment where art, nature, and imagination work together to support creativity, emotional awareness, and confidence., Through painting, gathering natural materials, myth-inspired storytelling, and reflective creative practices, children explore their own ideas in ways that honor individuality rather than comparison. There are no right or wrong outcomes — only discovery., Each child is welcomed as a Wonderkeeper — a young creator learning to care for their imagination, their ideas, and their sense of belonging., This camp is perfect for the dreamer, the quiet observer, and the child who has always had something to say but hasn’t yet found the way to say it. Small group sizes ensure every Wonderkeeper gets the attention, space, and encouragement they deserve., Our camps are led by experienced instructors who are passionate about nurturing young creative minds. We take great care to create a welcoming, inclusive environment where every child feels valued, supported, and free to be exactly who they are., Class minimum 4 participants/ 6 maximum.

events.description: Dive into the world of clay at our Creative Clay Creations Camp! Each session, students will explore the endless possibilities of stoneware clay to create functional and decorative pottery. With handbuilding techniques your child’s imagination truly has no bounds here., Throughout the week, our young artists will learn various techniques including extruding, slab building, texturizing, and pinch pottery. events.title: Playwriting Group
events.description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

events.title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
events.description: Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 5 participants

events.title: Rockin' the Gateway presents "Journey Revisited"
events.description: The sounds of Journey fills the stage a Western Gateway Park on August 1. If you're a fan of the legendary band Journey, then you simply can't miss Journey Revisited's electrifying live performances! Founded in 2011, this California-based band has dedicated itself to bringing back the classic Journey sound, and boy, do they deliver! With their exceptional musicianship and raw talent, they've managed to recreate the iconic sound of the album-oriented rock era, without the need for any pre-recorded backing tracks or digital vocal enhancements., These guys cover all the fan favorites, from the ever-popular Don’t Stop Believin’ and Wheel in the Sky to Any Way You Want It, Lights, and many more. But what sets Journey Revisited apart from the rest is their sheer energy and passion for the music. They pour their hearts and souls into every performance, and it shows. The audience can't help but feel the electrifying energy that fills the room, and it's an experience that stays with you long after the show is over. So, if you're in the mood for some classic rock music that will take you on a journey through time, then make sure to catch Journey Revisited in action.

events.title: Woodturning 101 – Craft a Beautiful Wooden Bowl
events.description: Are you interested in woodturning and looking to learn a new skill? In this class, you’ll learn how to turn a beautiful bowl out of wood using a lathe. The process is both rewarding and relaxing, as you take a piece of raw wood and turn it into a functional work of wood art. Under the guidance of our experienced instructor, you’ll start by learning the basics of wood lathe operation and safety. Once you’re comfortable with the equipment, we’ll move on to selecting and preparing the wood for turning. With the wood secured on the lathe, you’ll use a series of specially designed tools to shape the bowl.

events.title: Little Miss Sunshine
events.venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
events.description: 8/2 - An ensemble comedy masterpiece, The Onyx Downtown is excited to celebrate LITTLE MISS SUNSHINE for its 20th anniversary! The film follows the highly dysfunctional Hoover family as they travel 800 miles from Albuquerque, New Mexico to Redondo Beach, California, so their seven-year-old daughter Olive (Abigail Breslin) can compete in the "Little Miss Sunshine" children's beauty pageant. Along with Breslin, LITTLE MISS SUNSHINE boasts a cast that includes Toni Collette, Steve Carell, Greg Kinnear, and Alan Arkin. A critical darling and commercial success upon release, this road film still holds up today as a funny-yet-poignant examination of such themes as depression, self-acceptance, and finding meaning in the absurdity of life. Rated R.

events.title: Dailey & Vincent
events.description: Dailey & Vincent bring their acclaimed blend of bluegrass, country, and gospel to The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley on August 4, 2026. With multiple GRAMMY and Dove Award nominations, the duo is known for dynamic performances, soaring harmonies, and masterful musicianship that captivates audiences nationwide., This performance promises a night of storytelling, high-energy picking, and the artistry that has made Dailey & Vincent one of the most respected acts in contemporary bluegrass. Fans will enjoy a memorable evening of music that blends tradition and innovation.

events.title: "Musical Mornings" on Whimsical Wednesdays - Western Songbirds
events.description: Free to the public, Musical Mornings is a popular, family-friendly summer series of one-hour morning concerts for listeners of all ages. The atmosphere is casual, with attendees bringing their own lawn chairs or blankets and snacks. Kids are encouraged to wiggle, dance, sing, and play., The summer series, presented by InConcert Sierra, the Nevada County Library, and the Nevada County Friends of the Library, features local and regional musicians performing music geared toward youth yet enjoyable for all ages., The Western Songbirds is a beloved acoustic trio based in the Nevada City and Grass Valley area, featuring singer-songwriters Sands Hall, Maggie McKaig, and Sally Ashcraft. The eclectic ensemble brings together rich three-part vocal harmonies, storytelling, and an array of acoustic instruments.

events.title: Chris Dacre: INTER:reactions
events.venue_name: Center for the Arts | Granucci Gallery
events.description: Opening Reception: Friday., August 14, 2026 • 5-7pm, Exhibition Dates: August 6th- September 27th, 2026, Chris Dacre is a multi-disciplinary visual artist, educator and KVMRx radio deejay., These current artworks reflect three years of exploring painting as a medium. Moving between meticulous, reference-based painting and work created purely from imagination, I developed a visual dialogue across the pieces—a fragmented and often humorous exploration of the strange relationships between living beings and inanimate objects., From 2004 to 2017 I created work for my WAR is FUN! installations which transformed the heavy subject matter of war into large-scale, satirical environments influenced by my time in the U.S. Air Force. These works examine militarism, conflict, and the uneasy overlap between childhood play and adult warfare. I wanted to include some of that work in this exhibition because war continues to feel like a constant presence in our everyday lives.

events.description: Tickets: Reserved Seating | Genre: R&B, Soul, The Center for the Arts presents Corinne Bailey Rae – Like a Star: Celebrating 20 Years, a special anniversary tour honoring the songs that made her one of the most beloved voices in modern soul., The Grammy-winning artist behind global hits like “Put Your Records On” and “Like a Star” brings an intimate evening of music celebrating two decades of songwriting, storytelling, and timeless sound., A limited number of Put Your Records On VIP Experiences will also be available, offering fans the opportunity to meet Corinne Bailey Rae, watch a portion of soundcheck, and receive exclusive signed merchandise., VIP packages are extremely limited.

events.title: Stand by Me, 40th Anniversary
events.venue_name: The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre
events.description: 8/9 - The Onyx Downtown is proud to present Growing Pains, a new series focused on highlighting some of the most powerful and profound coming-of-age films ever made. Based on a Stephen King novella, the film follows four young boys who embark on a two-day trek into the Oregon wilderness to find the dead body of a missing child. Directed by Rob Reiner and anchored by terrific performances from its four leads (Wil Wheaton, River Phoenix, Corey Feldman, and Jerry O’Connel), STAND BY ME stands as a timeless ode to youthful nostalgia and a legendary coming-of-age classic 40 years since its initial release. Rated R.

events.title: Little Feat: The Last Farewell
events.description: Their inimitable blend of rock, New Orleans swamp boogie, jazz, and blues is not only unique but an ongoing source of delight. Feat has an incredible legacy of music and the celebration that comes with it, and the near future will see them honoring their own past with more playing. But it’s time to give the trusty tour bus, however comfy, a rest.

events.title: Night at the Grand Opera: A Giant Puppet Show
events.description: On August 11th at 7pm the Schoolhouse will have the incredible opportunity of welcoming String and Shadow's giant puppet theater to our outdoor amphitheater. The Olympia, WA theater company will join us for one night of their 2026 west coast tour, featuring their newest production ‘Night at the Grand Opera; A Giant Puppet Show'. , String and Shadow’s productions are a wonder to behold. This theater company really does it all, building larger than life sets and puppets from cardboard, fabric, and papier-mache. Their shows are written to entertain and engage all ages, and showcase a uniquely in-person art form that’s hard to come across in our increasingly digital world. You’ll love it, and you’re not going to want to miss it!, This is an ‘all or nothing’ fundraiser, meaning that if we cannot reach our goal by July 15th, the show cannot go on. , Please consider helping to back this show by purchasing ‘presale’ tickets for the event through our Kickstarter! We recommend a sliding scale donation of $20-$40 per person, which is roughly what a ticket would cost. , If you have more to give and want to ensure that the show will go on, consider a larger donation! If you can't afford a $20 donation, even $5 helps. , Got nothing to give? That’s fine! Tell your friends and loved ones and help spread the word., Once the event is funded, attendance will be ‘free’ and open to the public for everyone to enjoy.
